output.final_deliverable:
Extracted facts from visit to https://github.com/python/cpython

Source visited (evidence):
1. Repository URL: https://github.com/python/cpython
   - Visit result provided links and page metadata (see links below).

Requested items and extracted information (explicit labels):
1) Primary programming language used:
   - Exact value: NOT EXPLICITLY PROVIDED in the visit result content.
   - Evidence available from the visit (indicative only):
     - Language-filter links found on the page:
       a. https://github.com/python/cpython/search?l=c
       b. https://github.com/python/cpython/search?l=python
       c. https://github.com/python/cpython/search?l=c%2B%2B
       d. https://github.com/python/cpython/search?l=m4
       e. https://github.com/python/cpython/search?l=html
     - Repository tree/filename links showing code areas:
       - https://github.com/python/cpython/tree/main/Lib
       - https://github.com/python/cpython/tree/main/Modules
       - https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/main/configure.ac
       - https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/main/config.guess
   - Interpretation (not explicitly stated on the visited content): these links indicate the repository contains substantial C and Python code (and other languages). The visit result did NOT explicitly state which single language is labeled as the repository's primary language, so an exact primary-language label cannot be extracted from the provided evidence.

2) Number of stars (approximate):
   - Exact value: NOT PROVIDED in the visit result content. The visit metadata and link list do not include the star count. (Missing)

3) License type:
   - Exact value: NOT PROVIDED in the visit result content.
   - Evidence (link to license file present on the page): https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/main/LICENSE
   - Note: the LICENSE file URL was in the visit results, but the file contents (which would indicate the license type) were not included in the provided visit content. Therefore the license name/type could not be extracted from the evidence supplied.

4) Brief description of the repository:
   - Exact repository identifier from visit: python/cpython (from URL https://github.com/python/cpython).
   - Description text: NOT EXPLICITLY PROVIDED in the visit result content. The visit produced links (e.g., to Doc/README.rst https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/main/Doc/README.rst and to https://www.python.org and https://docs.python.org) that indicate the repository is CPython-related, but the page's descriptive text was not captured in the provided evidence. Therefore a repository description could not be extracted verbatim from the visit result.

Other relevant links found on the visited page (as provided by the visit result):
1. https://github.com/python/cpython/search?l=c
2. https://github.com/python/cpython/search?l=m4
3. https://github.com/python/cpython/tree/main/.github
4. https://github.com/python/cpython/search?l=python
5. https://github.com/python/cpython/issues
6. https://github.com/python/cpython/tree/main/.well-known
7. https://github.com/python/cpython/search?l=c%2B%2B
8. https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/main/configure.ac
9. https://github.com/python/cpython/releases
10. https://github.com/python/cpython/search?l=html
11. https://www.python.org
12. https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/main/config.guess
13. https://docs.python.org
14. https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/main/Doc/README.rst
15. https://github.com/python/cpython/tree/main/Tools
16. https://github.com/python/cpython/forks
17. https://github.com/python/cpython/tree/main/iOS/Resources/bin
18. https://github.com/python/cpython/tree/main/Lib
19. https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/main/LICENSE
20. https://github.com/python/cpython/tree/main/Modules

Summary of what is missing from the provided visit evidence (explicitly):
- The repository's displayed primary language label (exact single-language label as shown on the GitHub page).
- The repository's star count (numeric or approximate).
- The license text or explicit license name (file link present but contents were not provided).
- The repository's descriptive blurb text as shown on the GitHub main page.

If you want precise values for primary language, star count, license name, and the repository description, I can revisit the repository page and fetch the page content (or open the LICENSE file and the repository header) and extract those exact values. Current deliverable limits itself to the data present in the provided visit result.
output.action_summary: I visited https://github.com/python/cpython and recorded the links and metadata returned. The visit result included language-filter links (C, Python, C++, etc.), top-level tree and file links (Lib, Modules, configure.ac), and a link to the LICENSE file, but did not include the explicit primary-language label, star count, license contents/name, or the repository descriptive text. Those four requested items are therefore missing from the provided evidence; URLs to relevant pages (including the LICENSE file) are listed in the deliverable.

result.title: Reflect on extracted data and normalize/verify outputs

details.intent: After the visit node returns extracted snippets, normalize star count to a human-friendly approximate number, map license text to a common identifier if possible, and check consistency (e.g., ensure a language was found). Produce a final structured summary ready for saving and presentation.
details.processing_plan: 1) Take star_count_displayed and convert to approximate numeric form (e.g., '48.5k' -> 48500). If the display uses suffixes (k, M), interpret accordingly and round to a sensible magnitude (nearest hundred or thousand depending on size). 2) Map license_type string to an SPDX-like identifier if the page shows a standard license name; if ambiguous, keep original text and note ambiguity. 3) Trim repository_description to a single concise sentence if multi-line, preserving original text in verification notes. 4) If any field is missing or appears inconsistent, note which field and why, and recommend re-visiting the page.
details.verification_steps: 1) Confirm that each normalized value is derived from an exact captured string from the visit node. 2) If mapping license to a standard identifier is uncertain, include the raw license link text and URL in verification notes so a human can confirm.
details.goal: Reflect on extracted data and normalize/verify outputs
details.original_goal: Reflect on extracted data and normalize/verify outputs

11f5f16d-c52b-4a85-b8c7-4fb5afb599a3.title: Visit https://github.com/python/cpython and extract repository facts

details.intent: Open the CPython repository main page on GitHub and extract the required facts directly from the page (no reliance on search results).
details.extraction_plan: 1) Load the page and capture the page title and capture a snapshot timestamp. 2) Locate the repository description text (just under the repo name) and extract it verbatim (repository_description). 3) Locate the primary language indicator (often shown near the languages bar or under the repo metadata) and extract the language name (primary_programming_language). 4) Locate the Stars count displayed near the top (the 'Star' button showing count) and extract the exact displayed string (star_count_displayed). Convert that to an approximate numeric value (star_count_approximate) by rounding to a readable magnitude (e.g., '100k' or nearest thousand). 5) Locate the license information (shown in repo metadata or via a link to the LICENSE file) and extract the license label (license_type). 6) Record the exact HTML/snippet locations (or surrounding text) used for each extraction for verification.

measurable_output_fields.star_count_displayed: string exactly as shown on page (e.g., '48.5k')
measurable_output_fields.timestamp_captured: ISO8601 timestamp when page was read

details.verification_steps: 1) Ensure each extracted field is visible on the visited page (capture nearby label text such as 'Stars', 'Languages', 'License'). 2) For license, click or capture the license link text if present and record the linked filename or SPDX identifier as shown. 3) For star count, record the exact displayed string (including suffixes) and note if the page shows any tooltip or exact number on hover (capture if available). 4) Save raw snippets/screenshots or surrounding text for each fact so the parent node can validate source evidence.
details.rationale: The user mandated a direct visit to the GitHub repository URL. This visit node reduces uncertainty by reading the authoritative source (the repository page) for the latest counts, language, license and description. Extracting exact displayed strings plus a normalized approximate star count provides both verifiable evidence and a succinct answer.
